narrative_summary:
The BRCA2 missense change p.Ser2522Phe (also written S2522F) alters a serine residue in the C‑terminal region of the protein and has been reported in the context of rare cancers such as chordoma and as a germ‑line pharmacogenetic marker in hepatocellular carcinoma studies. Clinical reports have identified the variant in a chordoma patient whose tumor showed loss of the chromosome 13q region that harbors BRCA2, but the variant itself was classified as benign or likely benign in ClinVar and was not linked to a pathogenic phenotype in that cohort [PMC9444938]. The same variant appears in a database‑comparison study that highlighted its designation as a variant of uncertain significance (VUS) because of insufficient supporting data, reflecting the lack of consensus among public repositories [PMC4567983].

Functional investigations provide mixed signals. An HDR (homology‑directed repair) assay combined with ACMG/AMP criteria assigned the variant a likely‑benign (LBEN) classification, indicating that the amino‑acid substitution does not markedly impair BRCA2‑mediated DNA repair activity [PMC9433957]. Conversely, a pharmacogenetics review noted that Ser2522 lies within a helical domain critical for interaction with FANCD2 and listed the change among loss‑of‑function candidates, implying a potential pathogenic effect on protein function [PMC6689804].

No population‑frequency data or dedicated computational pathogenicity scores were presented in the extracted evidence, so allele‑frequency context and in silico predictions cannot be evaluated.

Taken together, the preponderance of evidence—particularly the ClinVar benign annotation and the HDR functional assay—supports a benign or likely benign interpretation of BRCA2 p.Ser2522Phe. However, the presence of a conflicting functional claim and the VUS designation in a database‑comparison study introduce uncertainty, indicating that additional functional validation and segregation data would be needed to resolve the discordance.

mentions.quoted_text: Given the close functional connection of BRCA2 with PALB2, we also analyzed BRCA2 rare NS variants in all familial and sporadic chordoma patients with WES data. We identified 11 rare NS BRCA2 variants in 10 sporadic chordoma patients (Table 2 and Figure 3A), all of which were technically validated by targeted Sanger sequencing. Two BRCA2 variants (NM_000059.4: p.Glu714Ala and p.Gly715Ter) were seen in the same patient, who was diagnosed with vertebral chordoma at age 51 years. The remaining variants were each seen in a single patient. Among the 11 variants, the majority result in a single amino acid change (Table 2). Eight of these variants are listed in ClinVar; p.Ile1173Phe and p.Thr2337Ile are considered VUS, p.Glu1593Asp, p.Lys1693Asp, **p.Ser2522Phe** and p.Asn2706Ser are benign or likely benign, and p.Cys1200fs and p.Arg2784Trp are pathogenic or likely pathogenic. The other three (p.Glu714Ala, p.Gly715Ter and p.Glu2301Gly) are novel and not listed in ClinVar. p.Gly715Ter is likely to be pathogenic given its protein-truncating nature and the fact that two other truncating variants (c.2137C>T, p.Gln713Ter and c.2147del, p.Gln716fs) in its vicinity are reported to be pathogenic in ClinVar. Similar to PALB2 variants, we also observed a higher genetic burden of BRCA2 rare variants in chordoma patients compared with controls (8% vs 3%, P=0.025).

Among chordoma patients included in our germline WES analysis, Formalin-fixed, paraffin-embedded (FFPE) tumor blocks or sections were available for 52 patients, including six patients carrying the germline rare variants in PALB2 (n=2) or BRCA2 (n=4). Copy number analysis of these tumors based on SNP array data showed that the deletion of chromosome 13q, where BRCA2 is located, occurred in 19 of 52 tumors (36.5%) and was one of the most frequent copy number events (Suppl. Figure S4A). Somatic mutations in chromatin remodeling genes (PBRM1, SETD2, and SMARB1) or CDKN2A homozygous deletions, which are the most common driver events in chordoma (Bai et al., 2021; Tarpey et al., 2017), were not found in the six patients carrying germline PALB2/BRCA2 variants. Tumors in two (p.Glu2301Gly and **p.Ser2522Phe**) out of four BRCA2 germline variant carriers with SNP array data available showed chromosome 13q deletions (Suppl. Figure S4B, S4C). The tumor in a PALB2 variant (p.Ser1165Leu) carrier, who was diagnosed with chordoma at 10 years old, contained a complete loss of chromosome 16, where PALB2 resides, as well as a copy number neutral loss of heterozygosity (LOH) of chromosome 13 (Suppl. Figure S4B, S4D). Interestingly, one tumor (the carrier of p.Glu2301Gly) had somatic deletions of regions containing all three genes, BRCA1, BRCA2, and PALB2. Although we were unable to determine whether the wt or the variant containing allele was lost, the somatic copy number data are generally consistent with the notion that PALB2 and BRCA2 genes may play an important role in chordoma development.

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: The article reports that the BRCA2 p.Ser2522Phe variant is classified as benign/likely benign in ClinVar and notes its occurrence in a chordoma patient whose tumor exhibited a chromosome 13q deletion, but no functional impact is demonstrated for this variant.
article_mentions.title: Rare germline variants in PALB2 and BRCA2 in familial and sporadic chordoma

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: The study finds considerable inconsistency in BRCA1/BRCA2 variant classifications among public databases, with the BRCA2 p.Ser2522Phe variant labeled as a VUS due to insufficient evidence.
article_mentions.title: Comparison of locus-specific databases for BRCA1 and BRCA2 variants reveals disparity in variant classification within and among databases

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: The article uses a homology‑directed repair functional assay combined with ACMG/AMP criteria to reclassify BRCA2 VUS, and classifies the S2522F variant as likely benign (LBEN).',
article_mentions.title: Classification of BRCA2 variants of uncertain significance (VUS) using an ACMG/AMP model incorporating a homology directed repair (HDR) functional assay

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: The review discusses chordoma therapeutic targets and lists several somatic variants, including BRCA2 p.S2522F, but provides no interpretation of this variant’s significance.
article_mentions.title: Emerging target discovery and drug repurposing opportunities in chordoma

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: The review summarizes pharmacogenetic biomarkers, including germ‑line variants, that may affect efficacy and toxicity of systemic therapies for advanced hepatocellular carcinoma, with emphasis on sorafenib and regorafenib.
article_mentions.title: Pharmacogenetics of the systemic treatment in advanced hepatocellular carcinoma
